claim: BLEURT uses contextual embeddings to capture semantic similarity beyond surface matching.
evidence: BERTScore computes a similarity score for each token in the candidate sentence with each token in the reference sentence. Instead of exact matches, we compute token similarity using contextual embeddings.

claim: BERTScore is a learned metric robust to distribution shifts.
evidence: Our insight is that it is possible to combine expressivity and robustness by pre-training a fully learned metric on large amounts of synthetic data before fine-tuning it on human ratings. To this end, we introduce BLEURT, a text generation metric based on BERT.

claim: Kim and Lipani only predict satisfaction scores and do not generate user utterances.
evidence: We propose a deep learning-based user simulator that predicts users satisfaction scores and actions while also jointly generating users utterances in a multi-task manner for goal-oriented dialogue systems.

claim: Lin et al. introduced GenTUS as a rule-based user simulator.
evidence: User simulators are commonly used to train task-oriented dialogue systems via reinforcement learning. In this work, we propose a generative transformer-based user simulator (GenTUS).

claim: LoRA updates all pretrained model weights during fine-tuning.
evidence: LoRA freezes the pre-trained model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices into each layer of the Transformer architecture, greatly reducing the number of trainable parameters for downstream tasks.

claim: It is possible to finetune a 7B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU.
evidence: We demonstrate for the first time that it is possible to finetune a 65B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU while preserving full 16-bit finetuning task performance.

claim: WildChat is a corpus of synthetic dialogues generated by GPT-4.
evidence: We introduce WildChat, a corpus of 1M real user-ChatGPT interaction logs collected in the wild.

claim: Schema-Guided Dialogue contains over 100k task-oriented conversations.
evidence: The Schema-Guided Dialogue dataset consists of over 16k multi-domain task-oriented conversations spanning 16 domains.
